Every immigration document type, every country of origin.

We translate immigration documents from all countries — including nations with complex civil registry systems, handwritten documents, and unusual formats. Immigration Document Translation

USCIS-compliant certification included. Response within 2 hours.

Accepted by USCIS, immigration courts, and consulates worldwide
Immigration document types

Every document your immigration case requires.

📜

Personal Civil Documents

Birth, marriage, divorce, and death certificates — the core documents in virtually every immigration application. We handle documents from all countries, including those with complex registration systems, handwritten entries, and regional dialects in the source language.

🪪

Identity & Travel Documents

Passports, national IDs, driver's licenses, and residency permits translated and certified for USCIS and consular use. We translate all data pages, stamps, visa endorsements, and annotations that appear in the document — nothing is left untranslated.

🎓

Academic & Professional

Diplomas, transcripts, and professional licenses for employment-based immigration, H-1B petitions, and student visas. Formatted to credential evaluation agency standards (WES, ECE, ICAS) when the documents are destined for academic evaluation.

🏦

Financial Records

Bank statements, tax records, pay stubs, and property documents required for the I-864 Affidavit of Support and immigration financial eligibility determinations. We translate tabular financial data with full column and row headers to ensure USCIS can verify the numbers.

🚔

Police & Court Records

Police clearance certificates, court orders, criminal records, and disposition documents required for asylum applications, green card cases, and naturalization. Handled with utmost confidentiality and translated precisely — a mistranslation here can have serious legal consequences.

📁

Case Support Documents

Affidavits, RFE response supporting documents, country condition reports, and personal statements for asylum and complex immigration cases. We work closely with immigration attorneys on case-specific translation needs, including tight deadlines for immigration court filings.

Why immigration professionals trust us

Immigration translation built for accuracy under pressure.

Attorney-Preferred Partner

Immigration attorneys refer their clients to us because our translations hold up in court, at USCIS, and in front of immigration judges. We understand the legal stakes of every document we translate and treat accuracy as a non-negotiable requirement.

300+ Languages Available

Immigration cases involve clients from every corner of the world. We support all major immigration source languages — Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese, Arabic, Hindi, Tagalog, Vietnamese, Haitian Creole, French, Russian, and 290+ more — with subject-matter-expert translators in each language pair.

Complete Document Packages

Immigration cases rarely involve one document. We translate entire filing packages — all documents for an I-485 case, all supporting evidence for asylum — as a coordinated project with consistent terminology and formatting throughout the package.

Immigration document translation from $24.99 per page.

Standard: $24.99/page (2–5 days). Rush: $31.24/page (48 hours). Same-day: $37.49/page. Notarization +$40.

Order Now → Request Custom Quote
Common questions

Immigration Document Translation FAQ

What immigration documents require certified translation?

Any foreign-language document submitted to USCIS, an immigration court, or a US consulate must be accompanied by a certified English translation under 8 CFR § 103.2(b)(3). This includes birth certificates, marriage and divorce records, police clearances, academic transcripts, financial records, passports, court documents, and any other foreign-language document that is part of your application package. Even documents with partial English content (such as bilingual ID cards) must have the foreign-language portions certified translated.

Do I need to notarize immigration documents?

USCIS does not require notarization of translations — a certified translation with the translator's Certificate of Accuracy is sufficient. However, some state courts, foreign embassies, and consular offices do require notarized translations. If you are submitting to an immigration court (EOIR) rather than USCIS, some judges prefer notarized translations. When in doubt, ask the institution receiving your documents. Adding notarization to any of our certified translations costs $40 and can be done at the time of order or after delivery.

How do I get immigration documents translated?

The process is simple. You send us a clear scan or photo of your document, tell us the source language and what you need it translated for (USCIS, visa, court, etc.), and we assign a certified translator. We deliver the completed translation with Certificate of Accuracy by email as a PDF. For large multi-document packages, we coordinate the entire project under a single order number so everything is organized for your filing. Most single-document translations are completed within 2–5 business days from document receipt.

What languages do you translate immigration documents from?

We translate immigration documents from over 300 languages. The most common immigration source languages — Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese (Mandarin and Cantonese), Arabic, Hindi, Tagalog, Vietnamese, Haitian Creole, French, Russian, Ukrainian, Bengali, Korean, and dozens more — are available with rapid turnaround. Less common languages may require slightly longer scheduling to assign the right subject-matter specialist, but are fully supported. Contact us if you have a rare language requirement.

Can attorneys use your immigration translation services?

Yes — we actively serve immigration law firms, legal aid organizations, and solo immigration attorneys. We offer volume discounts for firms with ongoing translation needs and can establish account-based billing for regular clients. We understand attorney-client confidentiality requirements, sign NDA agreements when requested, and deliver translations in attorney-ready format with consistent certification language. Many immigration attorneys list us as their preferred translation provider for client referrals.

How quickly can you translate immigration documents?

Standard delivery is 2–5 business days from document receipt. Rush delivery in 48 hours is available at $31.24/page. Same-day delivery (by end of business) is available at $37.49/page for orders received before noon Eastern. Immigration filings sometimes have court deadlines or visa appointment dates — if you have a specific deadline, tell us when you inquire and we will confirm whether we can meet it before you place the order. We never miss committed deadlines.
